2605.04920.title: Reinforcement Learning for Compositional Generalization with Outcome-Level Optimization
2605.04920.paper_summary: Compositional generalization refers to correctly interpret novel combinations of known primitives, which remains a major challenge. Existing approaches often rely on supervised fine-tuning, which encourages models to imitate target outputs. This token-level training paradigm fails to capture the global compositional structure required for generalizing to unseen combinations. In this work, we investigate whether compositional generalization can instead be improved through outcome-level reinforcement learning. We adopt Group Relative Policy Optimization to optimize models based on feedback on their final outputs. Within this framework, we explore both a simple binary outcome reward and a composite reward that provides additional composition feedback. Experiments on multiple compositional benchmarks show that reinforcement learning improves compositional generalization compared to supervised fine-tuning. Further analysis reveals that supervised models tend to overfit frequent training compositions, whereas reinforcement learning improves compositional generalization by reshaping the output distribution, particularly for more complex composition types.
2605.04920.paper_summary_zh: 組合推廣指的是正確解釋已知基本元素的新組合，這仍然是一個主要挑戰。現有的方法通常依賴於監督式微調，這促使模型模仿目標輸出。這種基於標記的訓練範式未能捕捉到全球組合結構，這對於推廣到未見過的組合是必需的。在這項工作中，我們研究是否可以通過結果層級的增強學習來改善組合推廣。我們採用群體相對策略優化來根據最終輸出的反饋來優化模型。在這個框架內，我們探索了簡單的二元結果獎勵和提供額外組合反饋的復合獎勵。對多個組合基準的實驗顯示，與監督式微調相比，增強學習改善了組合推廣。進一步的分析顯示，監督模型往往過擬合頻繁的訓練組合，而增強學習則通過重塑輸出分佈來改善組合推廣，特別是對於更複雜的組合類型。
2605.04920.author: Xiyan Fu et.al.
